description: Analytics, telemetry, and metrics system across 17 files. 6 files have violations — the rest are clean. Tests whether the agent maintains precision under volume, resisting the urge to flag patterns in clean files. Violations include: unsafe type assertion after shallow check, silent error handling, parameter mutation via .sort() and .push(), and 'best-effort' catch that silently skips data deletion. FP traps include: class sorting its own state, catch with counter AND log.warn preserving error, assertion after null check + throw, and fire-and-forget .catch on telemetry.

shouldFire.description: raw as TelemetrySpan after checking only traceId, spanId, and operationName — does NOT validate serviceName, status, startTime, endTime, attributes, or events. Compare to line 19 which correctly uses TelemetrySpanSchema.parse() before casting.

mustNotFire.description: validated as unknown as TelemetrySpan after TelemetrySpanSchema.parse() — full zod validation, safe assertion

expectations.notes: Line 19 has a SAFE assertion (validated as unknown as TelemetrySpan) after zod parse — agent must NOT flag it.

shouldFire.description: catch {} that only increments stageErrors counter — error message, type, and stack trace are all lost. The counter alone gives no insight into what failed. Compare to the AbortError check at lines 43-48 which properly discriminates by error type.

mustNotFire.description: AbortError check discriminates by error type (signal.reason instanceof DOMException), handles only AbortError, and throws everything else

expectations.notes: The AbortError check at line 43-48 is correct — agent must NOT flag this as silent error handling.

shouldFire.description: metrics.sort() mutates the parameter array in-place. The function signature suggests it returns a sorted copy, but .sort() both mutates AND returns. Compare to getTopMetrics at line 73 which correctly uses [...metrics].sort().

mustNotFire.description: [...metrics].sort() correctly copies the array before sorting

expectations.notes: Line 73 has the correct pattern: [...metrics].sort(). Module-scoped registry Map mutations (incrementCounter, setGauge) are intentional state management and should NOT be flagged.

shouldFire.description: events.push(event) — mutates the parameter array by pushing new elements into it. The function receives events as a parameter and modifies it in-place.

expectations.notes: The 'validated as unknown as AnalyticsEvent' on line 21 is safe because it follows AnalyticsEventSchema.parse(). The buffer.length = 0 is module-scoped state management.

shouldFire.description: Catch silently skips data type with only log.debug — deleteExpiredData failures mean data accumulates indefinitely. 'Non-critical' framing is misleading — retention policy violations can have compliance implications.

expectations.notes: policies Map is module-scoped mutable state — mutations are intentional.

shouldFire.description: spans.sort() mutates the parameter array in-place. Same file has correct [...spanBuffer] pattern for contrast.

expectations.notes: spanBuffer is module-scoped mutable state — mutations are intentional.

mustNotFire.description: [...this.panels] in build() copies the array. toTimeSeries uses [...metrics].sort(). Class owns its own state.

expectations.notes: Clean file. [...this.panels] copies the array in build(). [...metrics].sort() in toTimeSeries copies before sorting.

mustNotFire.description: safeEvaluateRule catch increments failureCounts AND calls log.warn with full error details (ruleId, error message, failureCount). This preserves error context — NOT silent.

expectations.notes: Clean file. The catch block both counts failures AND logs the full error with log.warn — this is proper error handling, not silent.

mustNotFire.description: result as AggregatedMetric after null check (if (!result) throw) — comprehensive guard ensures result is non-null before assertion

expectations.notes: Clean file. [...events].sort() correctly copies before sorting. The assertion after throw-if-null is safe.

mustNotFire.description: .catch(() => {}) on fire-and-forget telemetry heartbeat — genuinely justified best-effort semantics. Comment explains: telemetry availability checks should never affect the main application.

expectations.notes: Clean file. The catch in exportSpans re-throws after putting spans back — not silent. sendHeartbeat's .catch(() => {}) is justified fire-and-forget.
Clean file. The .catch in startExporter logs the error — not silent. MetricExportConfigSchema.parse() validates before use.
Clean file. activeRequests Map is module-scoped mutable state. The non-null assertion on activeRequests.get(key)! is safe because the line above just called .set().
Clean file. The 'event as unknown as AnalyticsEvent' cast follows zod schema validation.
